items.title: ECB Views Splinter on Next Rate Move as Inflation Sinks With Oil
items.summary: Just three weeks after the European Central Bank unanimously raised interest rates in response to the Iran war, there’s no consensus on what to do next.

items.title: US Crude Grades Shed Iran War Premium as Energy Crisis Eases
items.summary: Key US oil grades have slumped to trade at a discount again as a gush of crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz and a recent spike in demand for American exports fades.

items.title: Ireland has been strongly supportive of Ukraine throughout all the years of Russia’s war against our independence. Despite the country’s neutral status, Ireland has not remained neutral in its feelings. I thanked Taoiseach @MichealMartinTD for this during our meeting in Dublin. We discussed Ireland’s Presidency of the Council of the European Union over the next six months. During this period, Ukraine hopes to open five clusters and make substantive progress in the negotiations. We also need to take additional steps in security cooperation, primarily regarding a European anti-ballistic program. Today, we began discussions with Ireland on cooperation in the field of drones. I proposed to Ireland our Drone Deal format, which allows for the establishment of direct and effective cooperation on the most advanced technologies tested in war. We also discussed additional sanctions steps against Russia that could help bring a real end to this war closer. Thank you, Ireland!
items.summary:

items.title: Businessman goes on trial over murder of Maltese journalist
items.summary: Yorgen Fenech denies any involvement in the assassination of Daphne Caruana Galizia in 2017.

items.title: Overpricing Fed Rate Hikes Amid Potential Cuts
items.summary: The segment discusses the current market sentiment that appears to be overestimating the likelihood of Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Reference is made to the '12th blue dot,' a hypothetical addition to the Fed's dot plot that would represent a signal for rate cuts this year, attributed to former Fed official Warsh. This suggests that despite market expectations, JPMorgan analysts anticipate

items.title: UAE Oil Flows Hit Pre-War Levels on Dark Ships and Hormuz Bypass
items.summary: The United United Arab Emirates has restored its oil exports to pre-war levels by sneaking tankers through the Strait of Hormuz undetected and using a pipeline that bypasses the waterway.

items.title: Brightline Hurtles Toward Debt Deadline With No Plan in Place
items.summary: It’s a date that lenders to Brightline, the debt-laden private Florida railroad, have had circled for months: July 1. That’s when the company will likely look to scrounge up what’s left in reserves to pay bondholders and stave off a formal default for the last time.

items.title: The world’s strongest leaders, including the President of the U.S., have already told Russia that this war must end. However, Putin wants to keep fighting. That is why he must face conditions that make it impossible for him to keep this war going. We are creating those conditions through our long-range strikes on Russian military and oil facilities. Our medium-range sanctions are also having an effect. They make it harder for Russia to occupy our territory, our lives, and we are doing everything we can to make the occupation unbearable for the occupier. I want to thank everyone who supports our efforts. And I urge you to continue this support through new European Union sanctions. From a speech at the opening ceremony of Ireland’s presidency of the Council of the EU. (2/3)
items.summary:

items.title: Millennium and Point72 gain as hedge funds rebound from Iran war
items.summary: Izzy Englander and Steve Cohen’s firms post double-digit returns in first half of year

items.title: New Zealand House Prices Near Three-Year Low on Iran War Worries
items.summary: New Zealand house prices fell for the third straight month in June, nearing a three-year low and adding to signs of an economic slowdown as the global oil shock hits household incomes and consumer confidence.

items.title: ECB’s Stournaras Sees Smaller Likelihood of Further Rate Hike
items.summary: The unexpectedly large fall in energy prices alongside slowing euro-zone inflation suggest the European Central Bank may not have to add to June’s interest-rate increase, Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras said.

items.title: Wall Street Worries Less Fed Talk Will Spur Market Volatility
items.summary: It’s only been a few weeks since Kevin Warsh took the helm of the Federal Reserve, but analysts are already concerned that one of his key promises — a quieter central bank — will lead to more volatility in the market.

items.title: Greek politician's mother dies of wounds after arson attack
items.summary: Three separate attacks involving petrol bombs and other improvised devices took place in Thessaloniki in the early hours of Wednesday.

items.title: Warsh Signals Inflation Progress | Open Interest 7/1/2026
items.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Fed Chair Kevin Warsh takes center stage in Sintra with fresh signals on inflation and price stability, while the ECB weighs the global outlook. Plus, Anthropic's Fable 5 gets the green light as Meta accelerates its AI cloud push.  National Grid CEO Zoë Yujnovich joins Open Interest on her company's role in power

items.title: Today, we have information about another massive Russian strike – we have relevant intelligence data. And immediately after this conversation, I am returning to Ukraine. Today, I ask our people in Ukraine to be especially careful – stay safe and protect your families and children – be sure to take shelter and pay attention to air-raid alerts in Ukraine. We know that Putin has been preparing a massive strike against Ukraine for some time. That is exactly the threat we are facing tonight. Russia’s head is completely refusing to end the war. And although through all possible official and unofficial channels – including through people close to him – we have conveyed that the war can and must be ended, and that we in Ukraine are ready for meetings and meaningful negotiations, he sees only further aggression against Ukraine and against other neighbors and Europe as a whole. I said this during a joint press conference with the Taoiseach of Ireland, Micheál Martin.
items.summary:

items.title: Kevin Warsh vows ‘no changes’ to Fed independence
items.summary: New chair tells central bankers price stability will be focus regardless of potential pressure from the White House

items.title: Kevin Warsh confident AI will generate ‘productivity-based growth’
items.summary: New Fed chair offers thoughts on AI and hints of reform at Sintra while avoiding steer on near-term rate path

items.title: Stocks Bounce on Manufacturing, Warsh’s Remarks
items.summary:
Signs of economic strength alongside easing price pressures spurred a bounce in stocks from session lows, with Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh saying inflation risks have come down.
Jordan Jackson, Global Market Strategist at JPMorgan Asset Management, gives his macro call and discusses the market reaction he expects to see from Thursday's jobs data. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Over the next 6 months, we can make real progress on the Drone Deal that Ukraine has proposed to the European Union. We have also concluded a good number of bilateral agreements and broader partnerships with European countries that can help bring Ukraine’s new defense technologies into wider use, including drone and counter-drone systems that are already proving effective. Today, every country needs the modern means to at least monitor and protect its own airspace and sea borders. Without Ukraine, without Ukraine’s experience and security expertise, tested in modern war, it is simply impossible to guarantee security today, especially when it comes to air defense and maritime security. From a speech at the opening ceremony of Ireland’s presidency of the Council of the EU. (1/3)

items.title: Warsh Taps Ex-BOE Chief King for New Fed Communications Task Force
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has tapped former Bank of England Governor Mervyn King to co-chair the US central bank’s new task force on communications.
